content: since i got almost fucked up once, i didnt dare to copy paste using ctrl C, i just opened chatgpt from my main machine and wrote it down on my paper and type it out in the virtual machine where im taking the test

referenced_message.content: How did you copy and paste your questions into the VM without using the keys?

content:
hey @xaraye_50915, perhaps i wasnt clear enough with my statement, fyi, i do use evadus+ and did use the ctrl+alt+C, what i meant is dont use any commands other than the ones that are not included for the evadus+ controls like for my case:

i used a disable trackpad command: fn + f10, which caused SEB to detect me. 

other than that, youre fine with using the controls to exit the screen. such as ctrl+alt+C/ctrl+alt+F

content: How did you copy and paste your questions into the VM without using the keys?

referenced_message.content:
<@707511200765968464> yo g, I just took the test.

TLDR, it went well, evadus went undetected 

however, there was an instance where I disabled my track pad which caused SEB to detect suspicious activity and lock me out. A red screen will appear that says I’m locked and you have to type in a password to re-enter

And I almost fucked up there, but luckily the lecturer didn’t mess around with my laptop, he just asked me to do ctrl alt delete to prompt task manager to end the SEB task.

So what I did was close the VM, and restarted it. Then I had to request lecturer to allow me to re-attempt to unlock the SEB.

So in the end, I managed to get back in. If there’s any you shouldn’t do while in the SEB is to not do perform any shortcut keys other than the ones we used in evadus to minimise, etc. 

AVOID pressing fcntions such f10 as well as ctrl C

referenced_message.content:
Yes, this was tested with the latest version,

1. Yes, I haven't showcased it yet, but you can press ctrl + alt + f to maximize the VM, then it looks just like a normal PC if anyone looks your PC screen in person, pretty nice feature
2. No, in fullscreen mode it looks exactly like a normal pc
3. Yes, SEB is open source, so it's very easy to see that they haven't made any changes

Hi Bizzaro, just wanted to make sure whether the latest version of your evadus+ is working fine with SEB as of today?

Having a test tmr, and wanted to try it out, but concerned of getting detected by examiners using the VM.

and i referred to your latest video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W74bag2Erpw) on the SEB Demo, but need more clarification with the controls. 

question 1:  Is it possible to maximize the window of the SEB window in the VM itself like i circled in the image, so that in an actual scenario, i can quickly maximize the screen to hide from the examiner whos walking by to check. 

question 2: if i maximized the SEB exam screen, can i still see the windows toolbar below? cuz logically it shouldnt be there right? and do i just minimize by clicking the minimize key again? 

question 3: Just to confirm, i noticed that you mentioned respondus has been able to detect the VM earlier in your announcements and you have fixed it with a patch, question is can you confirm whether the current latest version of SEB cannot detect evadus+? the latest version for SEB is "Build 3.9.0.787"

Thats all, appreciate your help man. And sorry for being so detailed, i had to make sure whether it suits my usecase.
